title: Family Counseling * Hispanic/Latino Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Family Counseling * Hispanic/Latino Community 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Immigration Issues Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BALANCE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES BILINGUAL MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides bilingual mental health services. Specialties include trauma, prenatal and postpartum depression and anxiety, at-risk adolescents, depression, anxiety, parenting support, acculturation, and immigration issues. 11740 SW Warner Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on Hispanic and Latinx communities Hours: By appointment only Email (503) 596-2222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ENFOQUE DE LA FAMILIA COUNSELING CENTER COUNSELING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally-specific counseling with a focus on the Hispanic and Latino community for substance use, anger management, and/or domestic violence. Services are provided through group classes, family, individual, or marriage counseling, one-on-one problem-solving, and family activities. 205 SE 3rd Street Suite 200 Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Counseling: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Groups and classes: vary on weekday evenings (503) 681-4870 Get Directions More Details LIFESTANCE HEALTH LIFESTANCE HEALTH PORTLAND NE MLK CONEXIONES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers culturally and linguistically specific psychological evaluations and counseling. Evaluations are offered for cognitive functioning, attention problems (ADHD), disabilities, autism, and learning problems or disabilities for individuals from kindergarten age to college/university age. 3500 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Hispanic and Latinx identified individuals Hours: Front Desk: Monday-Friday 9am-6:30pm Appointments: Varies (503) 327-8205 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD EUGENE MAIN OFFICE MENTAL HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides bilingual mental health evaluations and services including individual, couples, and family therapy. 195 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Age 6 and older; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-6pm, after hours appointments available by request Email (541) 687-2667 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides bilingual mental health evaluations and services including individual, couples, and family therapy. 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Age 6 and older; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-6pm, after hours appointments available by request Get Directions Visit Website More Details BALANCE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES BILINGUAL MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES ENFOQUE DE LA FAMILIA COUNSELING CENTER COUNSELING CENTER LIFESTANCE HEALTH CONEXIONES PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD MENTAL HEALTH CARE PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD MENTAL HEALTH CARE
